items.expansion: Year To Date — performance from January 1 of the current year to today.
Month To Date — performance from the first of the current month to today.
Quarter To Date — performance from the first day of the current quarter.
Trailing Twelve Months — the most recent 12 months of data, rolling.
Last Twelve Months — synonym for TTM.
Fiscal Year — a company's accounting year (often not Jan–Dec).
Fiscal Quarter — a quarter within a company's fiscal year.
Month-over-Month — change vs. the prior month.
Quarter-over-Quarter — change vs. the prior quarter.
Year-over-Year — change vs. the same period one year ago.
Week-over-Week — change vs. the prior week.
Day-over-Day — change vs. the prior trading day.
Earnings Per Share — net income divided by shares outstanding.
Price-to-Earnings ratio — share price divided by EPS.
Price-to-Book ratio — share price divided by book value per share.
Price-to-Sales ratio — market cap divided by trailing revenue.
Price/Earnings-to-Growth ratio — P/E divided by expected EPS growth rate.
Enterprise Value — market cap plus debt minus cash; the cost to buy the business.
Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization.
Earnings Before Interest and Taxes — operating income.
Free Cash Flow — operating cash flow minus capital expenditures.
Capital Expenditures — money spent on long-lived physical assets.
Operating Expenses — day-to-day cost of running the business.
Cost of Goods Sold — direct cost of producing what is sold.
Return on Equity — net income divided by shareholder equity.
Return on Assets — net income divided by total assets.
Return on Invested Capital — operating profit (after tax) divided by debt + equity.
Weighted Average Cost of Capital — blended cost of a firm's debt and equity.
Discounted Cash Flow — valuation by discounting future cash flows to today.
Net Present Value — sum of discounted future cash flows minus initial cost.
Internal Rate of Return — discount rate that makes NPV zero.
Annual Recurring Revenue — annualized run-rate of subscription revenue.
Monthly Recurring Revenue — subscription revenue normalized to a month.
Gross Merchandise Value — total dollar value of goods sold via a platform.
Average True Range — measure of volatility based on recent price ranges.
Relative Strength Index — momentum oscillator (0–100), >70 overbought / <30 oversold.
Moving Average Convergence Divergence — trend-following momentum indicator.
Volume-Weighted Average Price — average price weighted by volume traded.
Time-Weighted Average Price — average price over a time window.
Moving Average — a smoothed average of price over N periods.
Simple Moving Average — equal-weighted MA.
Exponential Moving Average — MA that weights recent prices more heavily.
Bollinger Bands — a moving average ± N standard deviations.
Open Interest — number of outstanding derivatives contracts not yet closed.
Implied Volatility — the volatility implied by an option's market price.
Historical Volatility — realized volatility computed from past prices.
In The Money — an option with intrinsic value (would profit if exercised now).
Out of The Money — an option with no intrinsic value at the current price.
At The Money — strike approximately equal to the underlying price.
Days To Expiration — calendar days until an option or future expires.
Value at Risk — maximum expected loss over a horizon at a given confidence.
Conditional VaR — average loss in the worst-case tail beyond VaR.
Maximum Drawdown — largest peak-to-trough loss during a period.
Assets Under Management — total market value of assets a firm manages.
Net Asset Value — fund's total assets minus liabilities, per share.
Exchange-Traded Fund — a fund traded on an exchange like a stock.
Real Estate Investment Trust — pass-through entity that owns/finances real estate.
Initial Public Offering — first sale of shares to public markets.
Special Purpose Acquisition Company — shell that IPOs to acquire a private firm.
Mergers and Acquisitions — transactions where companies combine or are bought.
Leveraged Buyout — acquisition financed largely with borrowed money.
Private Investment in Public Equity — private placement of public-company shares.
Standard & Poor's 500 — index of 500 large US-listed companies.
Dow Jones Industrial Average — price-weighted index of 30 large US firms.
CBOE Volatility Index — 30-day implied vol of S&P 500 options ('fear index').
Federal Open Market Committee — sets US monetary policy.
Consumer Price Index — measure of consumer-level inflation.
Producer Price Index — measure of producer-level inflation.
Gross Domestic Product — total value of goods/services produced in a country.
Purchasing Managers' Index — survey-based gauge of business activity.
Non-Farm Payrolls — monthly US jobs report ex-agriculture.
Basis Points — 1 bp = 0.01%. 100 bps = 1 percentage point.
Foreign Exchange — the currency market.
